source: WSET SAT Level 3 + Marking Key patterns from official marking keys

quality_levels.description: Wine has identifiable faults that make it unpleasant or undrinkable
quality_levels.mentor_note: Only use if you have clearly identified a specific fault. Do not use vaguely.

quality_levels.description: Significant flaws or imbalances; lacks quality without being outright faulty
quality_levels.mentor_note: Rare in a WSET exam context as wines are selected to demonstrate identifiable styles

quality_levels.description: Straightforward wine with limited complexity; no obvious faults
quality_levels.mentor_note: Appropriate for simple, entry-level wines (e.g., basic Pinot Grigio from Veneto)

quality_levels.description: Good expression of variety and/or region; some complexity
quality_levels.mentor_note: Solid regional wine at mid-quality level

quality_levels.description: Clearly expressive, well-structured, some complexity; could improve with ageing
quality_levels.mentor_note: Model answer for Rustenberg Chardonnay (premium Stellenbosch) = Muy Bueno

quality_levels.description: Exceptional complexity, length, balance and expression; will develop further
signal_observations: carácter varietal y regional muy definido
quality_levels.mentor_note: Model answer for Ata Rangi McCrone Pinot Noir = Excelente. Reserve for wines of clear distinction.

quality_justification_requirements.principle: Quality conclusion must be supported by specific observations from nose and palate
common_errors: Stating quality level without linking to observed characteristics
Claiming 'excelente' without tertiary complexity or long finish

readiness_guidelines.demasiado_joven: Wine has high tannins/acidity not yet integrated; primary fruit dominant; little tertiary development
readiness_guidelines.drink_now_potential: Ready but will benefit from further cellaring; balanced; shows some development
readiness_guidelines.drink_now_no_ageing: Ready to drink; primary/secondary; limited ageing potential
readiness_guidelines.demasiado_viejo: Loss of fruit; oxidised; past peak; brown/brick colour; tired aromas
